dag_desc: Roadmap engine repo · r1 · skill-template hygiene + fleet-dashboard → roadmap-viewer migration. Plumbing-and-cleanup round: merge feat/stance-register-skills, sync templates with live skills, port the untracked roadmap-night template, register `roadmap viewer` as a first-class subcommand (PLAN-MODE expansion at runtime), refresh docs, capture LATE doctrine on §Skill-template-must-match-live and §Roadmap-viewer-as-first-class-subcommand. Narrower-by-design than fleet rounds — engine repo owns its own round, fleet's lane registry knits them.

tasks.desc: Merge feat/stance-register-skills → main. Open or update the PR (https://github.com/Ocean-Synaptics/roadmap/pull/new/feat/stance-register-skills), self-review the /stance-register diff against templates/skills/{roadmap-spec,roadmap-auto}.md, fast-merge with squash. If the branch is explicitly retained as a long-lived feat branch, write a named-successor reason into the receipt instead. Pre-commit hook blocks main; merge must go through the PR path.

validate.command: git log --oneline main | head -5 | grep -qi 'stance.*register\|register.*skills' || echo retained-as-feat-branch

tasks.desc: Port live ~/.claude/skills/roadmap-night/SKILL.md (179 lines) into templates/skills/roadmap-night.md. The file is currently untracked and a prior session left it there; this commits the synced version. Diff-empty between live and template after this node.

Diff every templates/skills/*.md against its corresponding live skill at ~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md. Produce a desync report: which templates need sync, which are diff-empty, which lack a live counterpart (templates that aren't installed). §Underused-IR-verb-audit analog applied to skills. Don't ship desync — open follow-up nodes if any survive.

Sync templates/skills/roadmap-auto.md against live ~/.claude/skills/roadmap-auto/SKILL.md (currently differs · likely the /stance register clause not yet round-tripped). Walk both directions: if live has clauses worth templating, pull them in; if template has clauses missing live, push out via /stance register or note divergence rationale.

Sync templates/skills/roadmap-spec.md against live ~/.claude/skills/roadmap-spec/SKILL.md (currently differs). Same walk as roadmap-auto: bidirectional, prefer-live for newly landed clauses, document divergence if any survives.

Templates carry rainbow banners + emoji headers per /stance form discipline (without /stance being the doctrine source — /stance is voice-not-roadmap-engine). Audit templates/skills/*.md for: (1) opening rainbow banner, (2) emoji section markers, (3) 💀 closer. Fix any drift. /stance walk-back narrowed INFECTION clause to human-facing only — the rainbow-banner styling discipline survives intact.

Locate fleet's roadmap-state dashboard code: /home/griffin/src/fleet/dashboard/ (confirmed exists). Inventory: components, data sources, build pipeline, deps, render entry. Output a manifest naming every file that must travel and every dependency that must be vendored or replaced. §Probe-before-anchor — don't assume structure.

PLAN-MODE parent: decompose fleet-dashboard → roadmap-viewer migration into executable sub-nodes based on the manifest. Expansion at runtime — exact sub-node count depends on what fleet's dashboard actually contains. Likely children: vendor-or-link-deps, port-render-entry, adapt-data-source-to-roadmap-context, register-cli-subcommand, smoke-test, docs. Plan-children-executor-doctrine applies: this node's executor ONLY decomposes, doesn't implement.

Register `roadmap viewer` as a first-class subcommand in src/cli/. Body may be a stub or first-page placeholder this round — the point is to claim the namespace and document the expansion path. `roadmap viewer --help` returns 0 and prints a meaningful help string. §Roadmap-viewer-as-first-class-subcommand lands here.

validate.command: node dist/cli.js viewer --help 2>&1 | grep -qi viewer || npx tsx src/cli.ts viewer --help 2>&1 | grep -qi viewer

tasks.desc: Make fleet's dashboard renderable from this repo. Either vendor (copy + license) the dashboard's runtime deps into this repo, or document the cross-repo link path with explicit pre-requisite. Decision recorded with rationale. §Fail-hard — pick ONE canonical path, no graceful both-paths.

Adapt fleet's dashboard data source (currently fleet-shaped roadmap state) to roadmap-engine context: read .roadmap/head.json, .roadmap/round-N/, .roadmap/trail.jsonl via existing roadmap CLI/lib functions. Pure adapter — fleet-flavored shape mapped to engine-flavored shape. Tests cover: empty round, partial round, completed round.

End-to-end smoke: `roadmap viewer` invoked against this repo's own .roadmap/ produces the expected first-page artifact (HTML or terminal output). The viewer doesn't have to be feature-complete — it has to render SOMETHING from real engine state without crashing. SHOW-DON'T-ASSERT.

Audit README.md against this round's skill changes and viewer registration. Update sections that mention skill list (now includes roadmap-night), CLI subcommand list (now includes viewer). Don't bloat — additive, surgical edits.

Audit CLAUDE.md (currently 21295 bytes — already over the conceptual 16K target). Acceptance scenario says ≤16K bytes; either trim toward target or explicitly defer with rationale. §No-doctrine-in-CLAUDE-md applies: pointer file, not doctrine library. Anchored-section mutation only.

validate.command: test $(wc -c < CLAUDE.md) -le 16384 || jq -e '.deferred == true and (.rationale | length > 0)' .roadmap/round-1/d-doc-audit-claudemd.json

tasks.desc: Audit ROADMAP_TOPOLOGY.txt against post-round state: skill list reflects roadmap-night addition, viewer subcommand registered, template/live sync invariant codified.

LATE-doctrine §Skill-template-must-match-live: every templates/skills/<x>.md MUST be diff-empty against ~/.claude/skills/<x>/SKILL.md at round-close. Test: `for f in templates/skills/*.md; do diff -q $f ~/.claude/skills/$(basename $f .md)/SKILL.md; done` returns clean. Empirical anchor: r1 — roadmap-night untracked + roadmap-auto/spec divergence. Author into the appropriate doctrine surface (NOT CLAUDE.md per §No-doctrine-in-CLAUDE-md).

LATE-doctrine §Roadmap-viewer-as-first-class-subcommand: visualization is part of the engine, not an external viewer. The viewer migrates from fleet-internal dashboard to `roadmap viewer` because the engine repo owns its own observability surface. Empirical anchor: r1 — fleet dashboard promoted. Captures §Sidecar-promotion-rule for the viewer specifically.

Bundle late-doctrine-r1 receipts. Both new §-anchors land in the roadmap repo's doctrine surface (skill or doc — NOT CLAUDE.md). Receipt cross-references load-bearing prior doctrine: §Spec-as-typed-knowledge-graph cluster, §Sidecar-promotion-rule, §Validator-as-process, §Plan-children-executor-doctrine, §No-doctrine-in-CLAUDE-md, §Doctrine-author-cannot-violate.

Final round survey: enumerate gaps remaining after this round (viewer is stub-not-feature-complete · any surviving skill desync · any deferred CLAUDE.md trim). Each gap gets a successor node nominated for r2. §Validator-as-process: every count claim paired with jq_probe_cmd.

Round terminal: convergence assessment, write successor (r2 scope: viewer feature-completeness + any spillover from gap-survey), close cleanly via /roadmap-term. Stratum-cadence n/a for this repo. Doctrine output goes to skills not CLAUDE.md (§No-doctrine-in-CLAUDE-md round-terminal clause).
